jokes.text: Wait, so you just painted your butt blue and nobody noticed the hole in your pants? - Yeah, it worked, it worked. - It's a good trick.
jokes.setup: Character casually mentions painting their butt blue as a solution to torn pants
jokes.punchline: Others treating this absurd solution as completely reasonable and clever
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how the gang normalizes completely insane behavior. Original visual absurdity with efficient setup.

jokes.text: The guy you went to high school with, who killed and ate his family? - Yeah... Yeah, yeah, yeah... but don't get all hung up on that, man
jokes.setup: Dee clarifies Pete killed and ate his family
jokes.punchline: Mac casually dismissing cannibalistic murder as something not to get 'hung up on'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Always Sunny dark comedy - treating horrific acts as minor inconveniences. Excellent character integration and quotable delivery.

jokes.text: they unscrambled his brain and he's, like, good now. - Yeah, they sorted him out, they sorted him out.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Pete's psychiatric treatment
jokes.punchline: Describing complex mental health treatment as casually 'unscrambling' a brain
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist description that shows the gang's complete ignorance of mental health while being quotable.

jokes.text: I lived with these maniacs in the loony bin. There is no curing those psycho bastards... but once your brain's a piece of shit, it's always a piece of shit.
jokes.setup: Frank speaking with authority about mental institutions
jokes.punchline: His crude, completely un-clinical assessment of mental illness
jokes.explanation: Frank's characteristically blunt and inappropriate take on mental health, delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: What'd you do, did you, like, yank it out in a fit of rage? That's pretty psycho... - No... no, it's just genetics. - Aw, genetics, so psycho...!
jokes.setup: Dennis asks if Pete pulled his hair out in rage
jokes.punchline: Mac calling genetics 'psycho' when Pete gives a normal explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of the gang's desperation to make Pete seem crazy. Mac's earnest 'so psycho' response to genetics is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: struggling with my weight for a while now, yeah; M-meds don't help. - Oh, oh, yeah, uh... - A psycho struggle!
jokes.setup: Pete honestly shares his weight struggles from medication
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately reframing normal problems as 'psycho'
jokes.explanation: Another great moment of Mac desperately trying to make Pete's normal problems sound crazy.

jokes.text: Actually, you know, j-just turn the music down, j-just a bit.
jokes.setup: Gang playing their 'freight train' song loudly for Pete
jokes.punchline: Pete's polite but clearly uncomfortable request to turn it down
jokes.explanation: Subtle cringe comedy showing Pete's discomfort with their nostalgic recreation.

jokes.text: Remember, like, you used to go right up to babies in carriages and just scream in their faces, you know, they'd cry...? - I-I don't feel good about that stuff. I actually regret a lot of the stuff we used to do.
jokes.setup: Mac fondly reminiscing about Pete terrorizing babies
jokes.punchline: Pete expressing genuine remorse while Mac recalls it as fun times
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Mac's nostalgic psychopathy and Pete's genuine growth/remorse.

jokes.text: This guy sucks now. - Oh, my God. - Like, I don't like him at all. - No, no, he's, like, not psycho at all. - No, it's like Mild Mannered Pete. - More like Boring Pete.
jokes.setup: Gang realizing Pete has become a normal, healthy person
jokes.punchline: Them being disappointed that he's no longer dangerously mentally ill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Always Sunny moment - the gang being upset that their friend got better. Dark, character-specific, and efficiently delivered.

jokes.text: Sorry about the stuff I was saying about, uh... the mentally insane... earlier. Did that anger you? ...I didn't mean to call... psychotic people 'mutants.'
jokes.setup: Frank attempting to apologize for his earlier insensitive comments
jokes.punchline: His 'apology' just reminds everyone of more offensive things he said
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank non-apology that makes things worse. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: the main problem with people going to these state-run loony bins is the separation between church and state... You spend most of your time talking to a therapist, instead of a priest.
jokes.setup: Mac about to explain a serious institutional problem
jokes.punchline: His completely backwards understanding of mental health treatment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac pseudo-intellectual moment showing his religious obsession and complete misunderstanding of therapy.

jokes.text: a priest is gonna let you off the hook for all the things that you've done... He needs to be absolved, so that he can go psycho again.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining the benefits of confession
jokes.punchline: His goal is to enable Pete to become a psychopath again
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Mac's twisted logic about using religion to enable dangerous behavior. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Holy shit, holy shit! - Oh, shit, what happened? Put it on, put it on. - Put it back on! Put it back on! What the hell happened to your face?!
jokes.setup: Gang asking Cricket to remove his hood
jokes.punchline: Immediate horror at seeing his burned face, desperate for him to cover it again
jokes.explanation: Strong visual gag with great immediate reaction comedy. Efficiently delivers shock and revulsion.

jokes.text: This happened to me when I was locked in your burning apartment at Thanksgiving. - No, you weren't there for that... - I don't think you were there, man.
jokes.setup: Cricket explaining how he got burned at their apartment
jokes.punchline: Gang denying he was there despite his obvious evidence to the contrary
jokes.explanation: Great example of the gang's denial and gaslighting, even when confronted with obvious consequences of their actions.

jokes.text: Why would we want you at Thanksgiving dinner? Especially looking like that, you would've freaked everyone out
jokes.setup: Dennis defending why Cricket wasn't invited to Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Using Cricket's current injuries (that they caused) as justification
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissistic logic - blaming the victim for the consequences of their own actions.

jokes.text: I don't need to suck him or anything or... let him inside me?
jokes.setup: Cricket being asked to hear Pete's confession
jokes.punchline: His immediate assumption it involves sexual acts
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate crude humor showing Cricket's degraded state, though relies somewhat on shock value.

jokes.text: if he wants up inside me, it will cost you a whole sixer, I mean, that... I got my dignity.
jokes.setup: Cricket discussing his pricing for sexual acts
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have dignity while literally prostituting himself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor about Cricket's delusional sense of dignity while discussing prostitution.

jokes.text: so they just cut all the loonies loose? - What? How could they do that? That's-that's insane! - I guess it was either that or raise our taxes.
jokes.setup: Gang discovering the mental institution was closed due to budget cuts
jokes.punchline: Frank calling it insane followed by immediate understanding when taxes are mentioned
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about selfish priorities and NIMBY attitudes toward public services.

jokes.text: What?! Raise my taxes? Come on. How much do these vultures need? I already pay a ton in taxes... I'm not paying more in taxes, Dee. I won't do it. Don't speak of it again!
jokes.setup: Discussion of raising taxes to fund mental health facilities
jokes.punchline: Frank's increasingly agitated refusal to pay more taxes despite the obvious need
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's selfishness and priorities, with nice escalating frustration.

jokes.text: It's okay, Froggy. I'll keep you moist.
jokes.setup: Frank talking to an imaginary frog friend
jokes.punchline: The tender but bizarre promise to keep the frog 'moist'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and quotable line that's both touching and deeply weird. Perfect economy of words.

jokes.text: Th-This is the nitwit school I was shanghaied to when I was a kid... That one that you had your mommy tell them to tell you that you didn't have donkey brains.
jokes.setup: Frank recognizing the mental institution
jokes.punchline: Referencing Charlie's certificate proving he doesn't have 'donkey brains'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to a fan-favorite running gag, delivered naturally within the story.

jokes.text: One time, we escaped and they chased us down with those big nets they use to catch dogs.
jokes.setup: Frank describing an escape attempt from the institution
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of them being caught with dog-catching nets
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist visual gag that plays into Charlie's earlier 'cartoon' comment about Frank's stories.

jokes.text: Did you grow up in a cartoon?
jokes.setup: Frank's increasingly absurd childhood stories
jokes.punchline: Charlie's perfect summarization of how ridiculous Frank's life sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on Frank's absurd backstories. Extremely quotable and efficient delivery.

jokes.text: they used that money to open up the Eagles' new practice facility... That makes sense. That's kind of a no-brainer... you can't have our guys out there twisting ankles... A man of Shady McCoy's abilities, he needs a proper landing strip.
jokes.setup: Learning tax money went to Eagles facility instead of mental health
jokes.punchline: Gang immediately agreeing this was the right choice and getting excited about sports
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing their warped priorities and Philadelphia sports obsession trumping basic human services.

jokes.text: Sophie's choice, huh? - Mm-hmm. - Oof. Go, Birds. - Go, Birds.
jokes.setup: Dennis calling the choice between mental health and sports facilities 'Sophie's choice'
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal and return to 'Go, Birds' chant
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy comparing a trivial funding choice to one of literature's most tragic dilemmas, then immediately dismissing it for sports.

jokes.text: Is that a glory hole? - Yes. It is, my son. But it'll cost you a whole sixer if you want to unburden yourself in that way.
jokes.setup: Pete noticing something in Cricket's confessional setup
jokes.punchline: Cricket confirming it's a glory hole and immediately offering services
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate shock humor that maintains Cricket's degraded priest persona while being cringe-funny.

jokes.text: he used to, um, scream in baby's faces. - Well, that's just funny. That was just plain funny... - Okay, no, you know what he used to do? He used to gather, like, cats and dogs... and then put them into like a little room and, like, have 'em battle it out. - That's just cats' and dogs' nature, to fight, so I don't see a problem with that.
jokes.setup: Gang trying to get Cricket to find Pete's actions sinful
jokes.punchline: Cricket justifying increasingly disturbing behavior as normal
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity as Cricket justifies objectively terrible behavior, showing his own moral degradation.

jokes.text: All right, um... he used to, um, scream in baby's faces. - Well, that's just funny. That was just plain funny. - I know, I know, I know. - They'd be all freaked out...
jokes.setup: Mac confessing Pete's sins for him
jokes.punchline: Cricket finding baby terrorizing 'just funny' instead of sinful
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing how corrupted Cricket has become - finding cruelty to babies amusing rather than sinful.

jokes.text: also, he murdered and ate his family. - Right. - What? - Jesus Christ! That guy's a goddamn maniac! I'm not absolving him!
jokes.setup: Cricket calmly justifying Pete's previous actions
jokes.punchline: His complete horror when he learns about the cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Cricket finally finds Pete's moral line after justifying everything else. Good reaction comedy.

jokes.text: there's a storm brewing on the inside... He's straight-up possessed by the devil... He used to be fun crazy... then the devil was like, 'I like this dude! I'm gonna get involved!'
jokes.setup: Cricket explaining Pete's mental state
jokes.punchline: His casual anthropomorphization of the devil as someone who gets enthusiastic about crazy people
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd theology from Cricket, treating demonic possession like casual social networking. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Get the demon, the goblin, the ghoul and then you got Pete. - Well, no, no, no, no. There's no goblins, there's no ghouls, it's just demons, okay? - Well, they're all made up, so...
jokes.setup: Mac enthusiastically listing supernatural entities to exorcise
jokes.punchline: Cricket's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that they're all fictional anyway
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment where Cricket undercuts Mac's religious enthusiasm with casual atheism despite being a 'priest.'

jokes.text: I'll act crazy and then he'll prescribe them to me. - Your bad acting will only confuse him and it will infuriate me.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his plan to get medication
jokes.punchline: His characteristic narcissistic concern about being personally infuriated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - making everything about himself even when discussing helping others.

jokes.text: Do you have some sort of donkey brain database? - No. - I'm looking for a kid, the frog kid. Just search for any half-man, half-amphibious creature.
jokes.setup: Frank asking the doctor for help finding someone
jokes.punchline: His completely serious request to search for a half-amphibian person
jokes.explanation: Great callback combination with Frank's characteristically absurd literal-mindedness about his childhood delusions.

jokes.text: I've always relied upon the kindness of strangers. - What is... what are you do... What is that, A Streetcar Named Desire?
jokes.setup: Dennis quoting Tennessee Williams as part of his 'crazy' act
jokes.punchline: Him immediately breaking character to critique the reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - his performative intellectualism breaking through even when trying to act crazy.

jokes.text: You haven't thought of the smell, you bitch!
jokes.setup: Dennis threatening to turn Dee into luggage
jokes.punchline: His sudden realization and passionate outburst about the practical flaw in his plan
jokes.explanation: Legendary Always Sunny moment. Perfect escalation from threat to practical concern. Became iconic quotable line showing Dennis's psychopathic attention to detail.

jokes.text: Of course I'm not, Dee. Don't be ridiculous. Think of the smell. You haven't thought of the smell, you bitch!
jokes.setup: Doctor asking if Dennis has a skin collection
jokes.punchline: Dennis dismissing it for practical rather than moral reasons
jokes.explanation: One of the best Dennis lines ever - the practical concern about smell rather than morality perfectly captures his psychopathy. Became a cultural reference.

jokes.text: ...you are Froggy. - Huh? - It was you, Frank! You are the frog kid!
jokes.setup: Frank desperately searching for his childhood friend Froggy
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he WAS Froggy all along - his imaginary friend was himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant plot twist that recontextualizes everything about Frank's childhood story. Perfect psychological reveal with great dramatic impact.

jokes.text: You were an outcast as a child, so you created an imaginary friend... But the lines of reality became blurred. Soon you didn't know where Frank began and Froggy ended.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining Frank's psychological condition
jokes.punchline: The clinical description of Frank's dissociative identity with an imaginary frog
jokes.explanation: Good psychological comedy that explains Frank's absurd backstory while maintaining the show's blend of clinical and absurd.

jokes.text: We have to catch the froggy. Put that down. Get out of here! - Here we go, come on... - I'm not sick anymore! I'm not a frog! - I know I'm not a frog! - Catch the froggy!
jokes.setup: Frank having an episode after the Froggy revelation
jokes.punchline: Frank chasing the doctor while the doctor insists he's not a frog
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Froggy revelation into physical comedy. The role reversal is well-executed.

jokes.text: Unless you have crack. If you have crack, let's boogie. - No one is going into your asshole. - I wouldn't let them without the crack.
jokes.setup: Cricket misunderstanding the exorcism plans as sexual
jokes.punchline: His casual negotiation about anal sex for drugs
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate crude humor showing Cricket's rock bottom, though relies on shock value more than wit.

jokes.text: Well, I had a simple conversation with a reasonable man and that man wrote me a prescription. - Mm, yeah. He got the drugs by being a real-life actual psychopath.
jokes.setup: Dennis proudly describing how he got Pete's medication
jokes.punchline: Dee pointing out he succeeded because he actually is mentally ill
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke about Dennis's actual mental state, with Dee delivering the brutal truth about his 'performance.'

jokes.text: All that stuff with my family? It's just, it's just a rumor people started about me... none of it was true, and then the more people spread it around, the more afraid people were of me.
jokes.setup: Pete finally explaining the truth about his supposed cannibalism
jokes.punchline: Revealing the gang's 'psycho' friend was just a victim of rumors and social anxiety
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion that reframes the entire episode - the 'psycho' was just anxious while the 'normal' friends are the real monsters.

jokes.text: Social anxiety disorder mixed with depression. I was really more of a danger to myself. I was always talking about committing suicide.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining why he was institutionalized
jokes.punchline: The dark reality that he was suicidal, not homicidal, while his friends expected a killer
jokes.explanation: Darkly subversive moment that adds real pathos while maintaining the comedy - Pete's actual problems vs. the gang's fantasies.

jokes.text: you always liked me, so I figured you guys could help me cope with it. - That we would help...? - Yeah! - Oh, yeah, yeah. Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining he came to them for emotional support
jokes.punchline: The gang's uncomfortable realization they're terrible people to ask for help
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment highlighting the contrast between Pete's genuine need and the gang's selfishness.

jokes.text: This thing's going all the way to Los Angeles, buddy, all right? City of Angels! Yeah, a place where no one is depressed. Yeah, you can surf the sadness away!
jokes.setup: Gang putting Pete on a train to get rid of him
jokes.punchline: Their completely wrong understanding of LA and mental health treatment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing their ignorance while disguising their selfishness as helping.

jokes.text: Oh, well, George, good luck. Enjoy Philadelphia, City of Brotherly Love. - Yeah. - They're gonna take great care of you here.
jokes.setup: Train conductor welcomes someone to Philadelphia
jokes.punchline: The ironic promise of 'brotherly love' and care after watching the gang abandon Pete
jokes.explanation: Dark ironic ending highlighting the contrast between Philadelphia's reputation and the gang's behavior.
